title: bryonia dioica var. acuta
intro: Bryonia dioica var. acuta, also known as White Bryony or Black Bryony, is a perennial climber native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant's roots, stems, and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal properties of Bryonia dioica var. acuta have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to support overall health and well-being.
